title: Gift Card Fraud Patterns
spl:
index=retail sourcetype="giftcard:event"
| search event IN ("ACTIVATE","REDEEM","TRANSFER")
| stats sum(amount) as flow, dc(card_id) as cards by customer_hash, _time
| bin _time span=1h
| where flow>5000 AND cards>10
| sort -flow
description: Detects bulk activation, balance draining, and reseller abuse patterns.
value: Detects bulk activation, balance draining, and reseller abuse patterns.
implementation: Velocity limits per device; block serial activations from single payment instrument across stores.
visualization: Table (suspicious bursts), Timeline (events), Bar chart (flow by hour).
knownFalsePositives: Gift card velocity from promotional loads, B2B incentives, and holiday campaigns; many bursts align with marketing calendars rather than fraud.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Gift card processor.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="giftcard:event"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Velocity limits per device; block serial activations from single payment instrument across stores.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=retail sourcetype="giftcard:event"
| search event IN ("ACTIVATE","REDEEM","TRANSFER")
| stats sum(amount) as flow, dc(card_id) as cards by customer_hash, _time
| bin _time span=1h
| where flow>5000 AND cards>10
| sort -flow
```

Understanding this SPL

**Gift Card Fraud Patterns** — Detects bulk activation, balance draining, and reseller abuse patterns.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="giftcard:event"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Gift card processor. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: retail; **sourcetype**: giftcard:event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=retail, sourcetype="giftcard:event". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by customer_hash, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• Filters the current rows with `where flow>5000 AND cards>10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (suspicious bursts), Timeline (events), Bar chart (flow by hour).
grandmaExplanation: In Retail and E-Commerce, we pay close attention to "Gift Card Fraud Patterns" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
